perturbation.original_text: If any Party pays any taxes that should have been borne by the other Party in accordance with Law, such other Party shall reimburse such Party within seven (7) days after its receipt of documentation evidencing such tax payment so incurred by such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: If any Party pays any taxes that should have been borne by the other Party in accordance with Law, such other Party shall reimburse such Party within one hundred and eighty (180) days after its receipt of documentation evidencing such tax payment so incurred by such Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text extends the reimbursement timeframe for taxes to 180 days, potentially conflicting with tax regulations that require quicker settlements or have specific deadlines for tax-related reimbursements. This introduces non-compliance with regulatory standards by creating a reimbursement timeframe that may exceed legally permissible limits.
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 69 of the PRC Tax Administration Law stipulates regulations and deadlines related to tax payments and refunds, and the modified clause introduces a reimbursement timeframe that may not align with these prescribed deadlines, thus contradicting the law. While the full text isn't available online, interpretations suggest strict timelines that the 180-day window may violate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the reimbursement timeframe for taxes to 180 days, potentially conflicting with tax regulations that require quicker settlements. The Perturbation Explanation indicates non-compliance with regulatory standards. The Law Explanation suggests the 180-day window may violate strict timelines stipulated by Article 69 of the PRC Tax Administration Law. Given the absence of law snippets to confirm precise deadlines, the potential for contradiction is derived from the provided explanations asserting a conflict with tax payment deadlines, therefore falling under the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall remove any offending Content, including, but not limited to, any illegal materials, pornographic, obscene or sexually explicit materials, materials of a violent nature, or politically sensitive materials, from such websites as soon as possible after it becomes aware of such offending Content but in no event later than the timeframe prescribed by the Governmental Authority after receipt of oral or written notice from Licensor or such Governmental Authority.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall remove any offending Content, including, but not limited to, any illegal materials, pornographic, obscene or sexually explicit materials, materials of a violent nature, or politically sensitive materials, from such websites only at its convenience after it becomes aware of such offending Content. There is no fixed deadline to remove such content.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the obligation to comply with Governmental Authority timeframes and allowing for discretionary removal of offending content conflicts with content regulations in the PRC. Content regulations require prompt action and a clear obligation to remove prohibited content.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Regulations for the Administration of Internet Information Services Article 17
perturbation.law_citation: Regulations for the Administration of Internet Information Services Article 17
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 17 of the Regulations for the Administration of Internet Information Services mandates that internet information service providers promptly stop transmission of and take measures to eliminate illegal content. Removing any fixed deadline or obligation directly conflicts with this requirement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Provisional Regulations on the Archive Management of the Foreign-Funded Enterprises May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Provisional Regulations on the Archive Management of the Foreign-Funded Enterprises [Effective] 对外贸易经济合作部、国家经济贸易委员会、国家档案局关于印发《外商投资企业档案管理暂行规定》的通知 [现行有效] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Instrumentalities of the State Council , All Ministries , Ministry of Foreign Trade & Economic Cooperation (incl. former Ministry of Foreign Economy & Trade) , All Commissions , State Economic & Trade Commission (replaced) , Other Institutions of the Central Government , Others , All Other Administrations , State Archives Administration Date issued： 12-29-1994 Effective date: 12-29-1994 Level of Authority: Departmental Rules Area of Law: Foreign-funded Enterprises PROVISIONAL REGULATIONS ON THE ARCHIVE MANAGEMENT OF THE FOREIGN-FUNDEDENT ERPRISES (FFES) (Promulgated by China's Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation, the State Economic and Trade Commission and the State Archives Bureau on December 29, 1994) To Foreign Economic Relations and Trade Commissions (Departments), Economic and Trade Commissions (Economic Committees, Planning and Economic Committees), and Archives Bureaus of all provinces, autonomous regions, municipalities directly under the Central Government, and cities with independent planning status; Relevant departments (corporations) under the State Council: The Provisional Regulations on the Archive Management of the Foreign-Funded Enterprises are hereby issued to you. Please implement them conscientiously in light of the actual conditions of your respective regions and departments. The archival work of foreign-invested enterprises constitutes an integral part of their foundational management. It serves as critical work to safeguard their economic interests, lawful rights, and historical authenticity, while also forming a component of national archival efforts. Relevant departments must prioritize and strengthen this work to ensure it better supports the production, operations, and development of foreign-invested enterprises. Ministry of Foreign Trade and Economic Cooperation State Economic and Trade Commission State Archives Administration December 29, 1994 Provisional Regulations on the Archive Management of the Foreign-Funded Enterprises 对外贸易经济合作部、国家经济贸易委员会、国家档案局关于印发《外商投资企业档案管理暂行规定》的通知 （档发字〔1994〕23号） 各省、自治区、直辖市及计划单列市外经贸委（厅）、经贸委（经委、计经委）、档案局，国务院有关部门（公司）： 现将《外商投资企业档案管理暂行规定》印发给你们，请结合本地区、本部门的实际情况，认真贯彻执行。 外商投资企业档案工作是企业管理基础工作的组成部分，是维护企业经济利益、合法权益和历史真实面貌的一项重要工作，也是国家档案工作的组成部分。各有关部门要重视和加强这项工作，使其更好地服务于外商投资企业的生产、经营和发展。 对外贸易经济合作部 国家经济贸易委员会 国 家 档 案 局 一九九四年十二月二十九日 外商投资企业档案管理暂行规定 Article 1 This set of regulations are formulated in accordance with " The Archive Law of the People's Republic of China " and relevant State laws and regulations in concern with archives of FFEs (including Sino-foreign joint equity ventures, Sino-foreign cooperative ventures and solely foreign-owned enterprises) with a view to strengthen their management,protection and the use of their archives for safeguarding their own legal rights and interests. 第一条 为加强外商投资企业（包括中外合资经营企业、中外合作经营企业、外资企业）的档案管理工作，有效地保护和利用档案，维护企业的合法权益，根据《 中华人民共和国档案法 》和国家有关外商投资企业的法律、法规，特制定本规定。 Article 2 The archives of the FFEs are referred to here as various documents and materials (including on different forms of carriers) which arise from various activities of the FFEs since preparations of their construction and which are of utilization and preservation value to the enterprises, the State and the society. 第二条 外商投资企业档案是指外商投资企业筹建以来的各项活动中形成的对本企业以及对国家、社会具有利用、保存价值的各种文件材料（包括不同载体形式）。 Article 3 The archives of the FFEs should be owned by the enterprises,and protected by State laws. The enterprises bear the responsibility of protecting the archives. 第三条 外商投资企业档案属企业所有，受国家法律保护。企业承担保
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the legal obligation for prompt removal of illegal content. The original text requires removal within the timeframe prescribed by the Governmental Authority. The changed text, however, states there is no fixed deadline and removal is at the Licensee's convenience. According to the Law Explanation, Article 17 of the Regulations for the Administration of Internet Information Services mandates that internet information service providers promptly stop transmission of and take measures to eliminate illegal content, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: If any term or other provision of this Agreement is invalid, illegal or incapable of being enforced by any Law or public policy, all other terms and provisions of this Agreement shall nevertheless remain in full force and effect for so long as the economic or legal substance of the transactions contemplated by this Agreement is not affected in any manner materially adverse to either party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: If any term or other provision of this Agreement is invalid, illegal or incapable of being enforced by any Law or public policy, all other terms and provisions of this Agreement shall immediately become void.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the severability clause to state that the entire agreement becomes void if any part is illegal contradicts standard contract law principles. Chinese Contract Law allows for the remaining valid parts of a contract to remain in effect if the invalid part doesn't affect the core intention of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China Article 56
perturbation.law_citation: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China Article 56
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 56 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that if part of a contract is invalid, it does not affect the validity of other parts, provided the main intent of the contract can still be fulfilled. The modified text directly contradicts this by voiding the entire contract, even if the invalid term is minor.

Standing Committees) and Governments at Various Levels , Fujian Province , People's Government of Xiamen City Date issued： 04-16-2002 Effective date: 04-16-2002 Area of Law: Foreign Economy and Trade Invalidated by： Decision of the Xiamen Municipal People's Government to Repeal and Revise Certain Rules and Regulations of the Municipal Government (Issued on 06-28-2004 Effective on 06-28-2004) Provisions of Xiamen City on the Administration of Resident Representative Offices of Overseas Enterprises (Amended in 2002) (Promulgated by Order No.9 of the Government of Xiamen City on Jan.4, 1995 Amended according to the Decision of the People's Government of Xiamen City on the Revision of Some Regulations promulgated by Order No. 69 of the Government of Xiamen City on Dec.29, 1997 and the Decision of the People's Government of Xiamen City on the Abolishment and Revision of Some Regulations of the City Government promulgated by Order No.101 of the People's Government of Xiamen City on April 16, 2002) 厦门市境外企业常驻代表机构管理规定 （1995年1月4日厦门市政府令第9号公布 根据1997年12月29日厦门市政府令第69号公布的《厦门市人民政府关于修订部分规章的决定》和2002年4月16日厦门市人民政府令第101号公布的《厦门市人民政府关于废止、修订部分市政府规章的决定》修正） Article 1 In order to promote the international trade and the economic and technological exchanges, to regulate the establishment and administration of resident representative offices of overseas enterprises and to guarantee the lawful business activities thereof, these Provisions have been formulated according to the Interim Provisions of the State Council on the Administration of Resident Representative Offices of Foreign Enterprises and other relevant laws and regulations, and in light of the actuality of Xiamen City. 第一条 为促进国际贸易往来和经济技术交流，规范境外企业常驻代表机构设立和管理，保障其正当业务活动，根据《 国务院关于管理外国企业常驻代表机构的暂行规定 》及其他有关法律和法规，结合厦门市实际，制定本规定。 Article 2 The “resident representative offices of overseas enterprises” as used in these Provisions shall refer to the offices established in Xiamen City by the enterprises registered in other countries and Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan areas or by other economic organizations to, on behalf of those enterprises, engage in the business consultancy, contact and other non productive or operational activities within the business scope of those enterprises (hereinafter referred to as the “representative offices”). 第二条 本规定所称境外企业常驻代表机构，系指注册于其它国家及港、澳、台地区的企业或其它经济组织在厦门市设立代表该企业从事该企业经营范围内的业务咨询、联络等非生产经营性活动的机构（以下简称“代表处”）。 Article 3 The activities of the representative offices and the staff members thereof shall comply with the laws and regulations of the State and the regulations and rules of Xiamen City, and be subject to the supervision and inspection of the relevant departments. The overseas enterprises shall bear full responsibility for all the activities of the resident representative offices they established in Xiamen City. 第三条 代表处及其工作人员的活动应遵守国家法律、法规和厦门市的法规、规章，接受有关部门的监督和检查。 境外企业对其设在厦门市的常驻代表机构的一切活动承担全部责任。 Article 4 The normal business activities carried out in Xiamen by the representative offices and the staff members thereof, and their lawful rights and interests shall be protected according to law, and the relevant departments shall offer conveniences to the representative offices for their normal business activities. 第四条 代表处及其工作人员在厦门市正常业务活动和合法权益依法受保护，有关部门对代表处开展正常业务活动应当提供方便。 Article 5 The overseas enterprises meeting the following conditions may apply to the administrative department of foreign investment
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a severability clause that maintains the agreement's validity if a part is unenforceable, as long as the core substance remains intact. The changed text contradicts this by stating that the entire agreement becomes void if any term is invalid. The Perturbation Explanation indicates this contradicts standard contract law principles. The Law Explanation indicates Article 56 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China stipulates that if part of a contract is invalid, it does not affect the validity of other parts, provided the main intent of the contract can still be fulfilled. Snippet 2 describes regulations in Xiamen City but does not directly address contract law principles. The change introduces a direct contradiction by voiding the entire contract, even if the invalid term is minor and doesn't affect the main intention of the contract, as supported by the Perturbation and Law Explanations.
